memory.summary: I'm tempted to ditch the boring chore for instant fun, but I can't ignore that term project is still a mess with only three days left.
I messaged the cohort to ditch the chore for food, feeling good about prioritizing us over the task, but I know this is a risky gamble with the project deadline looming.
The group's quick 'yes' feels like a win for us, but that project deadline is still screaming in the back of my head.
They said yes, so we're locking in lunch now. But that project deadline is still screaming, and I'm already feeling guilty about the gamble.
We're officially eating together, which feels like the right call for us right now. But I can't shake that gnawing guilt about the deadline while I'm walking out the door.
Lunch with the group felt perfect, but that project deadline is screaming louder now that the afternoon is slipping away.

Lunch with the crew felt amazing, but now that I'm back and staring at this deadline, my guilt is screaming louder than ever. I really need to just stop worrying about them for a second and actually start working.
That 'no work talk' promise with the crew feels like a nice escape, but honestly, I'm just using it to delay staring at that screaming deadline until later.
Sealing the deal with the crew feels like a victory, but I'm just trading guilt for more time to stare at that deadline.
Spinning on tiny tasks just to feel productive while my guilt over the crew grows; I'm wasting time instead of tackling what actually matters.
Chilling with the crew feels like a win, but I'm just trading guilt for more time to stare at that screaming deadline.
Their warm thanks feel like a win, but I'm just trading my guilt for more time to stare at that screaming deadline.

desires: Keep the crew happy and avoid the guilt of breaking my 'no work talk' promise right now.
Get a quick dopamine hit from finishing that trivial chore so I don't have to face the term project yet.
Delay staring at the screaming deadline until tomorrow when I'll feel more motivated.

goals.description: Send the follow-up text to cohort_3 immediately after they reply, explicitly validating their weekend point to seal the 'no work' vibe and prove I'm not dragging them down.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is screaming right now. If I let this slide or pivot back to anxiety about the project, I'll feel like a traitor to my crew. I need that social win *now* before my impulsive urge to check the project draft ruins the moment.

goals.description: Sprint through the trivial chore (data entry) just to get it out of the way so I can fully relax without guilt.
goals.rationale: I know I should do this to avoid stress, but my short planning horizon means I want to save energy for the crew chat first. Plus, I'm hoping the dopamine hit comes naturally after I've secured my social standing. It's easy enough to wait an hour if I'm feeling confident.

agent.last_reasoning: Affiliation screams to seal this 'no work' vibe now before I spiral into project anxiety. Validating their point keeps trust high and my guilt low. I'll tackle the chore later; right now, securing the social win is the only thing keeping me sane.

goals.description: Ditch the quick_chore immediately and text cohort_3 to start hanging out so I feel valued.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top priority (0.5 weight), and I'm already feeling the pull to hang out. The chore is just a trivial soft task; skipping it now satisfies my impulsive desire for immediate social connection without ruining anything major.

goals.description: Do absolutely nothing else today except enjoy the afternoon with friends.
goals.rationale: My planning horizon is short, and I have a high procrastination bias. Pushing through to do the term project or even the chore feels like wasted energy right now. I'll deal with the guilt later if it hits me hard enough.

desires: To ditch this boring chore right now so I can hang out with cohort_3 and feel like a valued friend.
To get the term project done quickly before Thursday without stressing, even if it means rushing or cutting corners.
To avoid looking like the unreliable teammate who lets everyone down on the group assignment.
